mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real retail sales reflects robust consumer expenditure, which translates into higher expected corporate revenues and earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is falsified if the average 20-day S&P 500 return following months with positive RSAFS growth is not significantly greater than zero (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate pinned at or below 0.25 percent signals aggressive monetary accommodation that suppresses the equity discount rate and incentivizes risk-taking, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is falsified if the average 60-day S&P 500 return is negative in more than 45 percent of months when FEDFUNDS is at or below 0.25 percent over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous deterioration in payrolls and rising unemployment indicates a contracting labor market that reduces aggregate wage income and consumer demand, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is falsified if the joint signal of falling PAYEMS and rising UNRATE fails to predict negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55 percent of triggered instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during early-cycle recoveries signals rising capacity utilization and operating leverage for manufacturing firms, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is falsified if positive 5-day S&P 500 returns occur in fewer than 52 percent of weeks when industrial production is rising during early-cycle periods over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 600 thousand units signal durable construction activity and upstream demand for materials, labor, and credit, which boosts cyclical corporate earnings and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is falsified if S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 40 percent of months when HOUST exceeds 600 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous acceleration in consumer prices and a federal funds rate below 0.5 percent compress real discount rates and squeeze corporate margins, expanding equity risk premia and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises > 1.0 point over 3 months and FEDFUNDS < 0.5
failure_condition: The hypothesis is falsified if the interaction of rising CPIAUCSL and low FEDFUNDS fails to produce negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in at least 50 percent of triggered months over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate of more than 0.3 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating household income and forward consumption, prompting equity de-risking and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is falsified if 5-day S&P 500 returns are positive in more than 50 percent of instances when the unemployment rate trigger is active over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP captures the broad trajectory of domestic economic output, with expanding output predicting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations align with macro reality.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is falsified if positive GDPC1 growth does not coincide with positive average 60-day S&P 500 returns over a 3-year rolling window, or if the Rank IC falls below 0.02.
